title: plantago lanceolata var. capitata
intro: Plantago lanceolata var. capitata, commonly known as narrow-leaved plantain or buckhorn plantain, is a perennial plant native to Europe, North America, and Asia, belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Plantago lanceolata var. capitata include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used externally to treat skin irritations, wounds, and minor burns.
